topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sterile fluorescent lights of the library hummed, a relentless, irritating sound that seemed to amplify the frantic drumming in my chest. I stared at the printed accusation, the ink blurring slightly under the harsh glare. My hands, usually steady when I was writing, were shaking so badly I had to grip the edge of the desk until my knuckles were white. Every word in the report felt like a physical blow, landing squarely in my stomach. I kept re-reading the highlighted passages, searching for a misinterpretation, a misplaced comma, anything to justify the accusation that hung heavy in the air around me. A cold sweat prickled my hairline despite the cool air conditioning. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the stacks, but my feet felt cemented to the floor.

[story 2]

"Are you sure about this?" Professor Albright’s voice was low, laced with an unshakeable disappointment that tightened the knot in my throat. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my mouth making the effort painful. I looked down at my notebook, the page filled with the research I had painstakingly compiled, and felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to crumple the paper into a tight ball. My gaze darted toward the exit, assessing the distance to freedom, but the sheer weight of the evidence kept me rooted. My breath hitched, shallow and quick, as I tried to formulate a response, but the words caught, lodging somewhere behind my ribs, refusing to surface.

[story 3]

The silence in my cramped dorm room was deafening, broken only by the rhythmic ticking of the cheap alarm clock. I paced the worn rug, the floorboards protesting beneath my hurried steps. Each turn of my head brought me back to the desk, where the digital file lay open—the damning proof. My jaw was clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my temples. I picked up my phone, intending to call someone, but my fingers hovered over the screen, paralyzed. A deep, unsettling tremor ran through my limbs, making it difficult to focus on anything beyond the stark reality staring back at me from the screen. I just stood there, breathing in short, shallow gasps.
